newsSinceJun6: No material fundamental news. TDK is showcasing AI data-center power solutions (8kW/12kW Infineon PSU reference designs, 'from grid to core' concept) at PCIM 2026 in Nuremberg June 9-11 - product marketing, not a contract or guidance event. The TDK Ventures investment in C2i Semiconductors (AI data-center power delivery) was announced May 27, before the window. Today's +3.23% move appears to be continued AI-passives sector flow, lifting YTD to +70.4% from ~65% with no new evidence.

sources.label: TDK at PCIM 2026 - AI data center power showcase (EE Journal)
TDK Ventures invests in C2i Semiconductors (Businesswire, May 27 - pre-window)

verdicts.rankRationale: Another +3.2% day on zero fundamental news pushes YTD past 70% on a crowding-sensitive residual-alpha list; the rerating is consuming the asymmetry and at $44B cap TDK is far less hidden than the Tier A names it sits above.
verdicts.bull: Direct bottleneck exposure across MLCCs, inductors, filters and AI power topology, with PCIM showing a full grid-to-core data-center power stack; HDD head volumes guided up ~20% add a second AI-storage leg.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact and execution fine, but nothing new since June 6; today's move is flow, so the alpha case is unchanged while entry price worsened.
verdicts.bear: 70% YTD with a 14-buy analyst skew means the passives-for-AI trade is now consensus; any AI capex digestion or FX reversal hits a rerated multiple hard.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q1 FY3/27 results and AI/data-center sales mix progress toward the 15%-of-sales FY2027 target
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowded AI-passives positioning unwinding on a capex-digestion scare after a 70% YTD run

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's verdict already marked TDK down for the YTD rerating; with no genuinely new information since June 6 and only a +3.2% flow-driven day, rank 55 on the broad board-level power and passives list still fits.
verdicts.bull: AI server PDN content (MLCCs, inductors, power components) plus HDD heads gives TDK multiple real AI revenue lines, and record FY2026 results confirm execution.
verdicts.neutral: PCIM showcase and the May C2i venture stake are incremental positioning, not contracts; the audit picture from June 8 is unchanged.
verdicts.bear: Large-cap recognition, consumer/auto cyclicality and FX exposure dilute the AI signal; further drift up the list requires hard design-win or capacity news, not trade-show demos.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Concrete AI data-center design wins or capacity commitments (e.g., PSU/PDN sockets) disclosed at or after PCIM
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical slowdown in consumer/auto segments masking AI content growth at a now-rerated valuation
